target.name: Mammoth Biosciences
target.founder: Jennifer Doudna, Trevor Martin, Janice Chen, Lucas Harrington

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $600M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $5.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.25B

data.narrative:
Mammoth Biosciences is a Series D-stage biotech company founded by Jennifer Doudna and others, having raised $600M+ to date. The valuation analysis incorporates a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public comparables in the Biotech & Health sector, including same-stage companies such as HistoSonics ($2.2B) and Formation Bio ($1.7B), as well as adjacent-stage peers like Abridge ($5.3B) and Ultima Genomics ($4.0B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier to the $600M total raised proxy, yielding $900M. Peer-Median analysis across six same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $2.3B, with a range of $1.5B to $5.3B. Public Comp Anchoring cannot be applied as target revenue is not disclosed. 

The analysis supports a valuation range of $765M (low) to $2.7B (high), with a median of $1.6B. The peer-median result sits materially above the stage-progression implied value, reflecting the capital intensity and founder profile of the target relative to other Series D biotech companies. Key uncertainties include target revenue run-rate, clinical development stage, and IP defensibility relative to the peer set.
